issues.title: Decorative images not properly marked
issues.description: Image with empty alt text should also have role="presentation" or aria-hidden="true" to properly convey decorative intent.
issues.impact: Screen reader users cannot understand non-text content
issues.recommendation: Add role="presentation" or aria-hidden="true" for decorative images with empty alt text.

issues.title: Missing captions for video content
issues.description: Video element is missing captions track. Deaf and hard-of-hearing users cannot access audio content.
issues.impact: Deaf or hard-of-hearing users cannot access audio content
issues.recommendation: Add a <track> element with kind="captions" and a valid .vtt caption file.
issues.codeFix:
<video controls>
  <source src="video.mp4" type="video/mp4" />
  <track kind="captions" src="captions.vtt" srclang="en" label="English" default />
</video>

issues.title: Missing audio descriptions for video
issues.description: Video element is missing audio descriptions track for blind users.
issues.impact: Blind users cannot access visual information in videos
issues.recommendation: Add a <track> element with kind="descriptions" to describe important visual information.

issues.title: Inaccessible SVG elements
issues.description: SVG element is missing accessible name. Screen readers cannot describe this graphic.
issues.impact: Screen reader users cannot understand non-text content
issues.recommendation: Add role="img" and aria-label to the SVG, or add a <title> element as the first child. Use aria-hidden="true" for purely decorative SVGs.
issues.codeFix:
<svg role="img" aria-label="Description of graphic">
  <title>Description of graphic</title>
  <!-- SVG content -->
</svg>

issues.title: Missing skip navigation link
issues.description: Page is missing a "Skip to main content" link. Keyboard users must tab through all navigation elements on every page.
issues.impact: Keyboard users must tab through all navigation on every page
issues.recommendation: Add a skip navigation link as the first focusable element in the page.
issues.codeFix: Skip to main content

issues.title: Focus visibility suppressed via CSS
issues.description: CSS removes focus outline without providing an alternative focus indicator.
issues.impact: Keyboard users cannot see which element has focus
issues.recommendation: Never remove focus indicators without providing a visible alternative (e.g., box-shadow, border, or custom outline).

issues.title: Auto-playing media content
issues.description: Media element auto-plays, which can be disruptive to screen reader users and users with cognitive disabilities.
issues.impact: Users with cognitive disabilities or vestibular disorders are affected
issues.recommendation: Remove the autoplay attribute, or provide a mechanism to pause, stop, or mute the media within 3 seconds.

issues.title: Duplicate element IDs
issues.description: Duplicate id="filter" found 2 times. IDs must be unique for ARIA associations (aria-labelledby, aria-describedby) to work correctly.
issues.impact: Assistive technologies cannot determine the purpose of UI components
issues.recommendation: Ensure each id value is unique within the page. Use classes for styling and unique IDs for ARIA references.

issues.title: Multiple h1 headings
issues.description: Page has 2 h1 headings. Use exactly one h1 per page for clear document structure.
issues.impact: Affects users relying on assistive technologies
issues.recommendation: Keep only one h1 that describes the page purpose. Convert others to h2 or lower.

issues.title: Hidden element still focusable
issues.description: Visually hidden element remains in tab order: <input type="hidden" name="family" value="wikipedia">
issues.impact: Affects users relying on assistive technologies
issues.recommendation: Add tabindex="-1" or aria-hidden="true" to hidden interactive elements.

issues.title: Link opens new window without warning
issues.description: Link "you can support our work with a donation." opens in a new tab/window without informing the user.
issues.impact: Affects users relying on assistive technologies
issues.recommendation: Add "(opens in new tab)" to the link text or aria-label.

issues.title: Missing skip navigation link
issues.description: Page does not have a skip-to-main-content link. Keyboard users must tab through all navigation on every page.
issues.impact: Detected during user journey testing â affects real user flows
issues.recommendation: Add a "Skip to main content" link as the first focusable element.

report.executiveSummary:
This accessibility audit evaluated the target against WCAG 2.2 guidelines. The overall accessibility score is 48/100, classified as "Non-Compliant". A total of 12 issues were identified (12 unique issue types) across 1 page(s). 

ð CRAWL COVERAGE: 1 of 1 discovered pages were audited (100% coverage). 

â ï¸ URGENT: 2 critical issue(s) require immediate attention. These block access for users with disabilities. 5 high-severity issue(s) significantly impact usability. 

ð¥ WIDESPREAD: 12 issue(s) appear on more than 50% of pages: "Missing captions for video content" (1 pages), "Focus visibility suppressed via CSS" (1 pages), "Inaccessible SVG elements" (1 pages).

ð¶ USER JOURNEY TESTS: 2/4 journeys passed. Failed: Keyboard Navigation, Error State Handling.

Category breakdown: Perceivable: 83/100, Operable: 72/100, Understandable: 98/100, Robust: 90/100.

ð¯ CONFIDENCE: 3 high, 9 medium, 0 low confidence issues.

journeyResults.description: Tests focus management in dialogs, dynamic content, and live regions.
Tests form accessibility including labels, grouping, autocomplete, and error handling.
Tests how the application communicates errors to assistive technology users.

steps.name: Error State Handling
steps.issue: No error handling patterns detected (no aria-invalid, no error containers)
